title: ZomboCom stolen by a hacker, sold, now replaced with AI-generated makeover
full_text:
This is a crime against internet history.

ZomboCom, the classic Flash-based website from the late 90s that told you that you could do anything at all with the only limit being 'yourself', was stolen by a hacker and then put up for sale on GoDaddy. And now, the site has been purchased and replaced with an AI-generated 'makeover' that completely loses the charm of the original. The logo for the new version of the site is AI-generated, and even worse, the original audio from the site has been replaced with a brand-new theme song generated using an AI program (e.g. Suno), it's an AI-generated melody that tries to capture the original's charm but falls flat on its face. And not only that, site has a progress bar on it that, once it hits 100%, you get the opportunity to 'Join Zombo'. The stupid part of this progress bar is that the original had a spinning, colorful circle, and so, the progress bar moving on the site defeats the entire purpose of the original Flash animation. The older version of this makeover was even worse; they didn't even try to make the logo look convincing even with being AI-generated, and the classic, spinning circle loading animation from the original site interpreted as a game show spinner. The absolute worst part is that the person or people currently in control of the site are trying to sell the site back to the original owner - yes, you heard me right, they're offering to give the site back to the original owner...at a price. And the original owner of the site has confirmed the site was stolen; they confirmed a hacker stole the site and put it up for sale, and they are in the process of trying to get the domain back.

This is absolutely evil and diabolical.
